MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James Picker, Esq. of Philip M. Andersen & Associates, T:925.225.6838, for defendant/moving party;
Larry Q. Phan, Esq. of Dreyer Babich Buccola Wood Campora LLP, T:916.379.3500, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was contested and argued. After arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT LIZA NAVARRO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, as follows:

Defendant Navarro's motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant knew or had reason to know of Mowgli's vicious propensities. (See Priebe v. Nelson (2006) 39 Cal.4th 1112, 1115-1116 ["If [defendant] knew or should have known of his dog's vicious propensities and failed to inform [plaintiff] of such facts, he could be found to have exposed [plaintiff kennel worker] to an unknown risk and thereby be held strictly liable at common law for her injuries. [Citations.] Under such circumstances, the defense of primary assumption of risk would not bar [plaintiff's] claim since she could not be found to have assumed a risk of which she was unaware."]; Plaintiff's Ex. 5 (Bhakri Depo., 20:8-24; 21:23-22:15; 24:10-12); Plaintiff's Ex. 7 (Grewal Depo., 18:8-19; 20:1-10); Wine Dec., 1-9.)

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
